Percentile EngineN/AN/AN/AN/AN/APercentile ranking (dashboard)
🔬 Ridge vs Lasso — Detailed Metrics
ModelOverall R²MAE (log)RMSE (log)MAE (USD)RMSE (USD)Pct Rank Err
Ridge0.83900.61130.7703$3,449,410$6,545,97710.06 pts
Lasso0.83910.61130.7700$3,499,530$6,623,57210.07 pts
Takeaway: Ridge and Lasso converge at R²≈0.839 — nearly identical performance. Ridge edges MAE ($3.45M vs $3.50M); Lasso edges RMSE log (0.7700 vs 0.7703). Percentile rank errors effectively tied (~10.06–10.07 pts). Both trail GradientBoosting (CV R²=0.879).
⚖️ Mean Absolute Delta % by Tier (Actual vs ML Predicted)
Interpretation: International tier has the highest error (103% mean |delta|) because salary variance is greatest there relative to model features. NBA Elite and Mid are more predictable once tier is known. The within-tier R² of 0.18–0.26 confirms per-game performance stats are the missing variable — they would meaningfully close this gap.

📊 League Comparison
📈 Position Analysis
🌍 Experience & Salary Relationship
🔍 Key Market Insights

Extreme Salary Inequality: Gini coefficient of 0.666 indicates a superstar-driven market where the top 10% of players earn 47% of total compensation.

Nationality Discrimination: 36.2% of international players earn less than US equivalents when controlling for position, age, and experience (t = -5.69, p < .001).

Position Premiums: Point guards command the highest salaries ($9.4M mean), followed by power forwards ($8.9M).

Experience Returns: Salary grows 6.4× from entry-level ($2.1M) to veteran status ($13.3M).
